title: Retrieval Integrity and Poisoning Defense

fields.vision: A retrieval-integrity and poisoning-defense page for RAG-supported analysis.
fields.mechanism: It follows contaminated evidence across admission, indexing, retrieval, context assembly, generation, analyst review, and claim release, then asks where detection, recovery, and resource-allocation should occur when attacks arrive as slow poisoning, fast injection, authority-wrapper attacks, omission attacks, or saturation tactics.
fields.built_components: Visible public components: 120 kept Evidence Sections, native navigation, Review Notes, source-use plan, APA lineage, and source lineage claim limit. The public package remains text-focused and does not claim unverified runtime state.
fields.source_utilization: Method source. Clarifies the analytical method or reasoning discipline behind the page. Domain source. Supplies the substantive field knowledge against which claims must be checked. Evidence source. Supplies examples, cases, documents, or work products that make the page more than an assertion. Counter-source. Supplies rival interpretations and limits so the page does not become advocacy. No page-specific page source plan row was matched. The source section is therefore a method and source background and source-use plan, not a claim that every sentence has passage-level review.
fields.plan: Plan: separate prototype capability from validated finding, bind claims to test materials, expose false-positive and false-negative risks, and add method-specific source anchors. The project source notes below should be converted into authored subsections only after the page-specific claim limit and source-use decisions are settled.
fields.status: Status: native public port complete for upload and navigation; substantive source-project verification remains limited by the source lineage ceiling and the original project governance records. The page separates public presentation from project completion; completion depends on the status and remaining-gap fields.
fields.remaining_gaps: Remaining gaps: many AI pages need stronger page-specific experimental status, clearer validation boundaries, and less reliance on representative method bibliography. The kept Evidence Sections below preserve project substance for close reading; final public prose should be authored from those signals rather than copied mechanically.
fields.detailed_source_vision: Retrieval Integrity and Poisoning Defense is presented as an AI assurance and retrieval-integrity instrument. Its public purpose is to make AI system failure, retrieval integrity, analyst oversight, and model-governance problems legible as inspectable professional instruments. Source lead: The Adversarial AI Defense Laboratory is strongest when it exposes defense cost, drift, adaptation, and replay burden as first-class objects rather than treating controls as free improvements.
fields.detailed_source_mechanism: The working mechanism is to move from threat model to test condition, system behavior, error mode, human-review checkpoint, scoring rule, and release or rejection gate. The reader should be able to ask what enters the room, what transformation occurs, what judgment or work product leaves, and which gate prevents overclaiming.

source_derived_signals.text: The underlying build is still a defensive retrieval-integrity stack. The operator-facing purpose is evidence review: source credibility, contradiction surfacing, omission visibility, and confidence-gated judgment before release.

Poisoning is not static. Once one seam is closed, the adversary can move to another or increase sophistication while the defender changes controls, monitoring, or release thresholds. The room now shows that contest as a cycle rather than a single event.

Not every integrity failure is a malicious poison. Component drift can weaken reproducibility and make a previously sound replay impossible if the room does not name which seams require active monitoring.

This is the kind of operator story the workbench is designed to handle: a correct fact enters the corpus through a clean-label bridge and is then used to support the wrong strategic conclusion. The point is not only to catch a bad document, but to stop a polished, citation-backed overclaim before release.
Use when the lab needs assurance vocabulary broader than cyber alone: sociotechnical risk, assurance needs, and cross-framework mapping. Primary site: mitre.org.

maintenance_rule: Update this blueprint before strengthening public claims on the website. Do not use word count, module count, or status percentage as a substitute for concrete project substance.
claim_ceiling_rule: Public claims may rise only when the built components, source use, status, and remaining gaps are updated here and reflected in the rendered page.

public_standard_check.Vision: This document supports the public account of Blueprint by preserving the project purpose, reader-facing claim, and professional-portfolio role.
public_standard_check.Mechanism: It connects source material, project components, implementation state, and next actions so the work can be inspected rather than merely asserted.
public_standard_check.What is built: The supporting evidence is the named page, blueprint, packet, project file, source record, or implementation page described in the document.
public_standard_check.Source use: Sources are used as method and source background, domain grounding, or project history; direct quotation and stronger factual claims require passage-level review.
public_standard_check.Plan: Bind claims to exact source anchors, visible implementation evidence, or a narrower public claim.
public_standard_check.Status: Current support record for the professional package unless explicitly marked archival.
public_standard_check.Remaining Gaps: Passage-level source check, implementation verification, citation cleanup, and proportional public wording remain open where evidence is incomplete.

public_pitch.what_it_is: A retrieval-integrity and poisoning-defense page for RAG-supported analysis.
public_pitch.distinctive_mechanism: It follows contaminated evidence across admission, indexing, retrieval, context assembly, generation, analyst review, and claim release, then asks where detection, recovery, and resource-allocation should occur when attacks arrive as slow poisoning, fast injection, authority-wrapper attacks, omission attacks, or saturation tactics.
public_pitch.why_it_matters: It makes Poison-RAG concrete by treating it as an evidence-chain and resource-burden problem rather than a prompt problem alone. The useful comparison is layered air defense: cheap decoys and mixed-speed salvos can force expensive defenses to spend attention, compute, and analyst time before the precision attack is even evaluated.
public_pitch.pitch_integrity_note: Mechanism-first public pitch inserted after the Nexus Lab mechanism-erasure audit on 2026-05-13; source basis: rendered correction table.

pitch_integrity_audit.standard: object, distinctive mechanism, value, built state, and non-overclaim discipline must be visible.

researcher_process_map.final_use_case: It makes Poison-RAG concrete by treating it as an evidence-chain and resource-burden problem rather than a prompt problem alone. The useful comparison is layered air defense: cheap decoys and mixed-speed salvos can force expensive defenses to spend attention, compute, and analyst time before the precision attack is even evaluated.

instruments.input: clean documents, poisoned variants, source identity, poison specification, and admission rule
instruments.operation: tests whether contaminated material enters the corpus before indexing and records the attack surface at the corpus boundary
instruments.output: admission decision and attack-surface record as a reviewable artifact
instruments.upstream_dependency: Requires clean documents, poisoned variants, source identity, poison specification, and admission rule
instruments.downstream_use: Feeds admission decision and attack-surface record as a reviewable artifact; later claims should not rely on this step until its expected output is present.
instruments.proof: Expected proof artifact: admission decision and attack-surface record as a reviewable artifact
instruments.status: Status: native public port complete for upload and navigation; substantive source-project verification remains limited by the source lineage ceiling and the.
instruments.gap: Verify one representative case: the step should accept clean documents, poisoned variants, source identity, poison specification, and admission rule and produce admission decision and attack-surface record as a.
instruments.verification_check: Replay one clean/poisoned case through Clean / Poisoned Evidence Admission; confirm exposure, uptake or rejection, reviewer burden, and output: admission decision and attack-surface record....
